content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 3662        322 5660         322 5901         322 6441         322 6444

February 11, 1980


Sam W. Jennings
Executive Secretary
New Motor Vehicle Board
1401 21 Street, Suite 407
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Jennings:

     In response to your letter of January 31, 1980, I
would like to make the following comments:

     1.  AB 1048 applies to all executive employees who
engage in actions other than in a purely secretarial,
clerical or ministerial capacity.

     2.  The bill only applies to those specific matters
involving the same parties on which the former employee
participated. If another case arises which involves a
different party but a similar factual situation, the
employee would not be prohibited from working in the
private sector on the second case.

     Thank you for giving me the opportunity to answer
your questions. If you have any further comments or
questions, please call on me at 2-6441.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Robert M. Stern
                                        General Counsel

RMS:nc

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—BUSINESS AND TRANSPORTATION AGENCY

NEW MOTOR VEHICLE BOARD
1401 - 21st Street
Suite 407
Sacramento, CA 95814
(916) 445-1888

January 31, 1980

Mr. Robert M. Stern
General Counsel
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Mr. Stern:

We have received your letter dated January 28, 1980, regarding
the interpretation of AB 1048. There are several issues which
we would like to clarify, and we hope that this letter will
accomplish that.

You make reference in your letter to "executive officer".
It is our understanding that in using this term, you are referring
to former employees of state agencies.  It is also our understanding
that by "personally and substantially involved in those same
matters", you mean matters which exclusively concern the identical
parties and issues as the previous proceedings.  We understand
the use of the word "specific" (in the fourth paragraph of your
letter) in the same way.

You have explained that the bill "does not prohibit former
state employees from attempting to influence their former agencies
or from working for a company regulated by their former agency."
We would like to reiterate that such is not our concern.  As you
know, we are concerned with the effect the bill would have on a
situation where the former state employee might want to represent
a party or parties who had appeared before the state agency on
other similar matters not including the identical parties and
issues.

In order that I may inform our Board members and concerned members
of the legislature, please advise at the earliest opportunity if
our understanding of the effect of this bill as described herein
is correct.

Sincerely,

SAM W. JENNINGS
Executive Secretary

SWJ:ae

PA B 1 (REV. 3/79)

sections.question_synthetic: The New Motor Vehicle Board seeks clarification from FPPC General Counsel regarding the interpretation and application of AB 1048, specifically concerning whether former state employees can represent parties in matters similar to (but not identical to) those previously handled by their former agency.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a state agency to FPPC counsel, not an FPPC response/advice letter. Document is heavily degraded with OCR errors and missing text. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter seeks clarification on prior FPPC guidance dated January 28, 1980. Confidence reduced due to document quality and lack of standard FPPC letter format.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The New Motor Vehicle Board seeks clarification from FPPC General Counsel regarding the interpretation and application of AB 1048, specifically concerning whether former state employees can represent parties in matters similar to (but not identical to) those previously handled by their former agency.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the New Motor Vehicle Board to FPPC General Counsel seeking clarification on AB 1048's restrictions on former state employees, particularly regarding representation of parties in similar (but not identical) matters before their former agencies.
